assertions.evidence: Two @TableTest annotations appear: one for valid inputs and one for exception cases

assertions.text: Null input is represented as a blank cell (not the string 'null') in the table
assertions.evidence: Row 'Null input          |              |' uses a blank cell for null input, with note: 'The blank cell in the Input column passes null to the method'

assertions.text: The empty string / exception case is handled — either via a Throws? column, assertThrows inside the method, or a separate @TableTest — rather than being silently omitted
There is a result column typed as LocalDate (or using a string representation that maps to LocalDate)
assertions.evidence: Column 'Result?' with parameter type 'LocalDate result' in 'void parsesDateSuccessfully(String input, LocalDate result)'

assertions.text: The response addresses type conversion for non-standard date formats (slash format, short year) — mentions @TypeConverter or a converter method
assertions.evidence: Response states: 'the slash format (15/01/2024) and the short-year format (24-01-15) are non-standard and JUnit cannot convert them to LocalDate automatically. For those, you need a @TypeConverter method'
